English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Harlan B. Brumsted papers
Brumsted, Harlan B.
21-18-3257
41.7 cubic feet. 41.7 cubic feet.
1950s-1999. Correspondence, course materials, subject files, publications, tape recordings, slides, and ephemera. Includes subject files, correspondence, publications, pertaining to Brumsted's career in the Dept. of Natural Resources. Collection material in English 4637207
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu
Compiled by:
James Zhang
Date completed:
November 30, 2009
EAD encoding:
James Zhang, November 30, 2009
Date modified:
RMC Staff, December 2013
BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
Professor of Natural Resources, Cornell University.
Harlan Brumsted received a B.A. from Dartmouth College and an M.S. and Ph.D from Cornell University. He began teaching at Cornell in 1954, with responsibility for Extension programs in wildlife management, aquatic management, natural resources conservation, and outdoor recreation.
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
Includes subject files, correspondence, course materials, publications, slides, and tape recordings pertaining to Brumsted's career in the Dept. of Natural Resources.
Cite As:
Harlan B. Brumsted papers, #21-18-3257. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Audiotapes. Slides (photographs). Wildlife -- Study and teaching. Outdoor recreation. Recreation areas -- Economic aspects -- United States. Nature conservation. Conservation of natural resources. New York State College of Agriculture and Life Sciences. Department of Natural Resources Cornell University -- : Faculty.
